---
title: "Cross-sell pairs"
description: "Suggest complementary items on the item detail page — pair pizza with sparkling water, dessert with coffee."
sidebarTitle: "Cross-sell"
icon: "arrows-split-up-and-left"
---

Cross-sell pairs help diners discover dishes that go together. When a
diner taps an item, Rumizi shows a small "Goes well with" section
underneath with the items you paired.

## Adding a pair

<Steps>
  <Step title="Open the Cross-sell tab">
    In the menu editor, swipe to the **Cross-sell** tab.
  </Step>
  <Step title="Pick a source item">
    The source is the item a diner is *already viewing*. Pick the item
    you want to suggest pairings from.
  </Step>
  <Step title="Pick a target item">
    The target is what shows up in "Goes well with". Pick one or more.
  </Step>
  <Step title="Save">
    Save the tab — like every editor tab, nothing is final until you
    save.
  </Step>
</Steps>

## Pairs are directional

This matters: *Margherita → Sparkling water* does **not** also add
*Sparkling water → Margherita*. You can suggest the drink from the pizza
without cluttering the drink page with pizza suggestions.

If you want both directions, add two pairs.

## Good patterns

- **Mains → drinks.** Diners are already buying the main; suggest the
  drink that fits.
- **Desserts → coffee/tea.** A natural upsell at the end of a meal.
- **Starters → mains.** Less common — diners are usually choosing their
  own main — but useful for tasting menus or recommended pairings.

## What diners see

On the item detail page, below the main content, a short row of small
cards with photo + title + price. Tapping one navigates to that item.
